message:
fix: correct agent type translation, path parsing, and log message

Three bug fixes across robot, tracker, and resilience modules:

internal/robot/robot.go - Agent type translation:
- Problem: determineState() passed full agent type (e.g., "claude")
  to status.DetectIdleFromOutput() which expects short codes ("cc").
- Solution: Add translateAgentTypeForStatus() call before detection.
- Fixes false-positive idle detection for agents with full type names.
- Affected: robot-status, robot-tail output state determination.

internal/tracker/tracker.go - Git quoted path handling:
- Problem: Git status outputs paths with spaces/special chars in quotes,
  e.g., "\"path with spaces.txt\"". Old code only stripped outer quotes.
- Solution: Use strconv.Unquote() to properly decode escaped sequences.
- Handles: spaces, unicode, embedded quotes, tabs, etc.
- Falls back to simple quote-stripping if Unquote fails (non-UTF8).
- Fixes file tracking for projects with unusual filenames.

internal/resilience/monitor.go - Auto-rotation log message:
- Problem: Log said "Auto-initiating rotation" but rotation requires
  user interaction (browser account switch), so nothing actually happened.
- Solution: Change message to accurately say rotation requires user
  interaction and provide the command to run manually.
- Prevents confusion when users expect automatic rotation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ import (
 	"os"
 	"os/exec"
 	"path/filepath"
+	"strconv"
 	"strings"
 	"sync"
 	"time"
@@ -508,8 +509,12 @@ func SnapshotGit(root string, opts SnapshotOptions) (map[string]FileState, error
 
 		// Handle quoted paths from git (e.g. "file with spaces.txt")
 		if len(relPath) >= 2 && strings.HasPrefix(relPath, "\"") && strings.HasSuffix(relPath, "\"") {
-			relPath = relPath[1 : len(relPath)-1]
-			// TODO: Handle escaped characters inside quotes if necessary
+			if unquoted, err := strconv.Unquote(relPath); err == nil {
+				relPath = unquoted
+			} else {
+				// Fallback: just strip quotes if unquote fails
+				relPath = relPath[1 : len(relPath)-1]
+			}
 		}
 
 		fullPath := filepath.Join(root, relPath)
